title: How to create your own Radio Station using a dynamic ip domain.
author: Dimitris Tzemos
summary:
In previous posts “Steps to configure Dehydrated for ZeroSSL and Let’s Encrypt” and “Steps to configure Dehydrated for ZeroSSL and Let’s Encrypt for One IP and multiple domains”
We saw how we can create a certificate for our domains.

Now will see step by step how we can create our own radio station using a dynamic domain and the certificates we had created.
I try to be as simple as i can and most work can be done by copy and paste.

First have to create a dynamic domain e.g. on noip.me lets use for this "how to" the foo.ddns.net

we have to open on our router the ports 80 for httpd, 8000 for icecast, 443 for ssl, 8443 secure port for icecast

I have build for salix 64 bit the packages because packages i use for slackel do not run on salix of course.

Download and i
full_text:
In previous posts “Steps to configure Dehydrated for ZeroSSL and Let’s Encrypt” and “Steps to configure Dehydrated for ZeroSSL and Let’s Encrypt for One IP and multiple domains” We saw how we can create a certificate for our domains. Now will see step by step how we can create our own radio station using a dynamic domain and the certificates we had created. I try to be as simple as i can and most work can be done by copy and paste. First have to create a dynamic domain e.g. on noip.me lets use for this "how to" the foo.ddns.net we have to open on our router the ports 80 for httpd, 8000 for icecast, 443 for ssl, 8443 secure port for icecast I have build for salix 64 bit the packages because packages i use for slackel do not run on salix of course. Download and install icecast package, libigloo and install rhash sudo slapt-get -i rhash Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Download and install mpd package Download and install mympd package Optional install libmpdclient sudo slapt-get -i libmpdclient Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode and Download mpc and install it Replace the /etc/icecast.xml with this Greece admin 100 2 524288 30 15 10 65535 hackme hackme admin hackme foo.ddns.net 1 8000 /live UTF-8 /live UTF-8 8443 /live UTF-8 1
/usr/share/icecast /var/log/icecast /usr/share/icecast/web /usr/share/icecast/admin access.log error.log 3 10000 0 nobody nogroup /etc/icecast2/bundle.pem Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode you can change the /live with /whatever_mount_you like Create the certification key for icecast secure connection.The fullchain.pem and privkey.pem we have created on previous posts “Steps to configure Dehydrated for ZeroSSL and Let’s Encrypt for One IP and multiple domains” or “Steps to configure Dehydrated for ZeroSSL and Let’s Encrypt” for foo.ddns.net, have to be copied in bundle.pem (we use it in icecast.xml above in line /etc/icecast2/bundle.pem mkdir -p /etc/icecast2 cat /etc/dehydrated/certs-letsencrypt/foo.ddns.net/fullchain.pem /etc/dehydrated/certs-letsencrypt/foo.ddns.net/privkey.pem > /etc/icecast2/bundle.pem chown nobody:nogroup /etc/icecast2/* chmod 600 /etc/icecast2/* Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Set up mpd client. Copy this to /etc/mpd.conf music_directory "~/music" playlist_directory "~/.mpd/playlists" db_file "~/.mpd/database" log_file "~/.mpd/log" pid_file "~/.mpd/pid" state_file "~/.mpd/state" sticker_file "~/.mpd/sticker.sql" ## Replace myuser with yours mpd has to run as current user and no root user "myuser" # For network bind_to_address "localhost" port "6600" restore_paused "no" metadata_to_use "artist,album,title" auto_update "yes" default_permissions "read,add,control,admin" audio_output { type "shout" encoder "vorbis" name "My Radio" host "localhost" port "8000" mount "/live" password "hackme" bitrate "128" format "44100:16:2" description "Greek - Rock" # optional genre "Greek GREEK greek Greece greece" public "yes" mixer_type "software" } filesystem_charset "UTF-8" replace in the line user “myuser” the “myuser” with your “username” you log in. name “My Radio” description “Greek – Rock” # optional genre “Greek GREEK greek Greece greece ” with whatever you like Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Set up mympd web UI client for mpd. I have created a script which automatically set up mympd with your radio domain. sudo RADIO="https://foo.ddns.net" sh /usr/src/mympd/config-helper.sh Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Set apache Edit /etc/httpd/httpd.conf Be sure the lines to be uncommented Listen 80 LoadModule proxy_module lib64/httpd/modules/mod_proxy.so LoadModule proxy_http_module lib64/httpd/modules/mod_proxy_http.so LoadModule proxy_wstunnel_module lib64/httpd/modules/mod_proxy_wstunnel.so LoadModule rewrite_module lib64/httpd/modules/mod_rewrite.so LoadModule ssl_module lib64/httpd/modules/mod_ssl.so Include /etc/httpd/extra/httpd-vhosts.conf Include /etc/httpd/mod_php.conf Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode And this line to be commented #Include /etc/httpd/extra/httpd-ssl.conf Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Also look for these lines to be as bellow. DirectoryIndex index.html index.php Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Edit /etc/httpd/extra/httpd-vhosts.conf and replace all with these lines # Virtual Hosts # # Required modules: mod_log_config # If you want to maintain multiple domains/hostnames on your # machine you can setup VirtualHost containers for them. Most configurations # use only name-based virtual hosts so the server doesn't need to worry about # IP addresses. This is indicated by the asterisks in the directives below. Listen 443 SSLCipherSuite HIGH:MEDIUM:!MD5:!RC4:!3DES SSLProxyCipherSuite HIGH:MEDIUM:!MD5:!RC4:!3DES SSLHonorCipherOrder on SSLProtocol all -SSLv3 SSLProxyProtocol all -SSLv3 SSLPassPhraseDialog builtin SSLSessionCache "shmcb:/var/run/ssl_scache(512000)" SSLSessionCacheTimeout 300 #### ServerAdmin webmaster@foo.ddns.net DocumentRoot "/srv/httpd/htdocs" ServerName foo.ddns.net ErrorLog "/var/log/httpd/foo.ddns.net-error_log" CustomLog "/var/log/httpd/foo.ddns.net-access_log" common RewriteEngine On RewriteCond %{HTTPS} off RewriteRule (.*) https://%{HTTP_HOST}%{REQUEST_URI} #RewriteRule ^(.*)$ https://%{HTTP_HOST}%{REQUEST_URI} [R=301,L] DocumentRoot "/srv/httpd/htdocs/" ServerName foo.ddns.net:443 ErrorLog "/var/log/httpd/foo.ddns.net-error_log" CustomLog "/var/log/httpd/foo.ddns.net-access_log" common SSLEngine on SSLCertificateFile /etc/dehydrated/certs-letsencrypt/foo.ddns.net/cert.pem SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/dehydrated/certs-letsencrypt/foo.ddns.net/privkey.pem SSLCertificateChainFile /etc/dehydrated/certs-letsencrypt/foo.ddns.net/chain.pem RewriteEngine On ## --- myMPD Configuration --- ## Automatically add slash if user writes /mympd, then redirect to /mympd/ ## The [R=301,L] makes a permanent redirect and stops processing here RewriteRule ^/mympd$ /mympd/ [R=301,L] ## 1. myMPD Interface # We want a password for user admin, to not let anyone to log in to mympd UI # Password set with "sudo htpasswd -c /etc/httpd/.htpasswd admin" AuthType Basic AuthName "myMPD Control" AuthUserFile /etc/httpd/.htpasswd Require valid-user ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:8081 ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:8081 # 2. myMPD WebSockets (Necessary for UI) ProxyPass ws://127.0.0.1:8081/ws ProxyPassReverse ws://127.0.0.1:8081/ws # 3. Στατικά αρχεία (Required for page rendering) ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:8081 ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:8081 Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Create a password for user admin so only admin (you) can log in to mympd web UI sudo htpasswd -c /etc/httpd/.htpasswd admin Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Start httpd (apache) sudo killall httpd sudo service start httpd Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode check if httpd is running ps ax |grep httpd Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode 4759 pts/2 S+ 0:00 grep httpd 12079 ? Ss 0:03 /usr/sbin/httpd -k start 12080 ? Sl 0:03 /usr/sbin/httpd -k start 12082 ? Sl 0:03 /usr/sbin/httpd -k start 12136 ? Sl 0:02 /usr/sbin/httpd -k start 12304 ? Sl 0:04 /usr/sbin/httpd -k start Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Start icecast sudo service start icecast Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode start mpd sudo service start mpd Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode start mympd sudo service start mympd Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode check if icecast is running ps ax |grep icecast Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode 1610 ? Sl 0:23 /usr/bin/icecast -c /etc/icecast.xml -b Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode we see icecast is running using /etc/icecast.xml config check if mpd, mympd are running ps ax |grep mpd 1714 ? Ssl 14:40 /usr/bin/mpd /etc/mpd.conf 1791 ? S 0:00 su -s /bin/sh -c /usr/bin/mympd -w /var/lib/mympd -a /var/lib/mympd/cache mympd 1793 ? Ssl 0:00 /usr/bin/mympd -w /var/lib/mympd -a /var/lib/mympd/cache Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode we see mpd is running using /etc/mpd.conf config file and mympd is running also as user mympd which created from config-helper.sh script above. check in which ports icecast, mpd, mympd are listening (I got these from my icecast, mpd, mympd used for my radio station to show the output of commands) sudo netstat -anpt | grep 'icecast' we see icecast is listening on 8000, 8443 from outside 127.0.0.1:8000 is our inside port tcp6 0 0 :::8000 :::* LISTEN 1610/icecast tcp6 0 0 :::8001 :::* LISTEN 1610/icecast tcp6 0 0 :::8444 :::* LISTEN 1610/icecast tcp6 0 0 :::8443 :::* LISTEN 1610/icecast tcp6 0 0 192.168.1.210:8443 85.75.211.110:43202 ESTABLISHED 1610/icecast tcp6 0 0 127.0.0.1:8000 127.0.0.1:39658 ESTABLISHED 1610/icecast Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode 192.168.1.210:8443 is my local ip to secure port 8443 and 85.75.211.110 is my dynamic ip i get from my host provider this is the ip of my meraklis.ddns.net domain so when user type on firefox https://meraklis.ddns.net as we see bellow they are listening to my “Meraklis Radio” or typing https://meraklis.ddns.net:8443/live on vlc or smplayer or exaile they llisten to “My Radio” station. Same for http://meraklis.ddns.net:8000/live unsecured connection used by https://dir.xiph.org/ radios directory sudo netstat -anpt |grep mpd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:6600 0.0.0.0:* LISTEN 1714/mpd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:6600 127.0.0.1:50884 ESTABLISHED 1714/mpd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:39658 127.0.0.1:8000 ESTABLISHED 1714/mpd Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode we see that mpd is listening in its default port 6600. And port 8000 send to icecast media server the streaming to go outside to internet sudo netstat -anpt |grep mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 0.0.0.0:* LISTEN 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:34994 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:52924 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:56132 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:33188 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:50882 127.0.0.1:6600 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:50884 127.0.0.1:6600 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:38972 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:35334 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:56130 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:41094 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:35022 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:38970 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:8081 127.0.0.1:34992 ESTABLISHED 1793/mympd Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode we see mympd is listening to internal port 8081 we do not need this port to be opened If you have created you playlists then typing on Firefox https://foo.ddns.net/mympd you will see mympd interface from where can add playlists or songs to play in random or single mode etc Your stream url is https://foo.ddns.net:8443/live or insecure http://foo.ddns.net:8000 https://foo.ddns.net:8443 goes to icecast web interface where can log in as administrator using username:admin and pass:hackme typing https://foo.ddns.net/mympd on you mobile phone can control your radio from where you are. also for listen from you phone type https://foo.ddns.net:8443/live or for icecast web type https://foo.ddns.net:8443 or http://foo.ddns.net:8000 These https://foo.ddns.net:8443/live or http://foo.ddns.net:8000/live can be used on vlc or smplayer or exaile to listen to your radio from outside on internet. To have users to listen to your radio without to type https://foo.ddns.net:8443/live just https://foo.ddns.net copy these to /var/www/htdocs/index.html ####### index.html ############ My Radio - Live Rock, folk | foo.ddns.net

The radio for music lovers

Welcome to My Radio, the station that honors good singing.

https://hosted.muses.org/mrp.js

My Radio
Now it's playing...
Loading...
Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode 17.1 copy the lines bellow to /var/www/htdocs/proxy.php proxy.php shows a transparent rectangle where the title of current playing song appears Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode To see how this index.html is looking and also the result of all these above click in this linkMy Radio to listen to “My Radio” we have created. To see the “My Radio” running on my laptop, i have changed the foo.ddns.net to meraklis.ddns.net and also i discovered that running two instances of icecast, mpd and mympd with different configurations can have two stations to stream different songs under the same dynamic ip. Furthermore can create some php pages and a mysql database where listeners can send you radio orders to play. If anyone is interesting can tell me to send or post here the php files and details. You can see the admin_radio_orders page in the admin_orders-img2.png admin_orders-img3.png on the screenshots link bellow. The two radio stations My Radio created for this post and Meraklis Radio are running on my lenovo laptop 8GB RAM and from a 64GB USB stick where slackel is installed (real installation), two years ago, which upgraded to latest updates almost every day because of slackware current tree. More screenshots "My Radio" station we created streaming MyMPD web UI icecast media server web UI
